beats.text: Here's a three-by-three matrix A — rows starting with one, three, and zero.
A highlight marks the first pivot: the entry in row one, column one. That's our anchor.
Row two becomes row two minus three times row one. Watch the three in row two, column one vanish — replaced by zero. The rest of row two updates with it.
Repeat down the matrix. Each step zeros out one entry below the pivot. The system tidies itself, level by level, until the matrix stands upper triangular — call it U.
Now back substitution. The last equation has one unknown — solve it. Climb up row by row, plugging each answer in, until all three unknowns are found.
Every elimination step is secretly a matrix multiplication. An elementary matrix E appears beside A, recording each move. String them together and you hold the full architecture of the solution.
